item.title: Travere Filspari Launch
item.description:
• Summary: Travere Therapeutics has launched Filspari (sparsentan) for the treatment of IgA nephropathy, a rare kidney disease. Analysts project the drug could achieve blockbuster sales due to its first-in-class mechanism as a dual endothelin and angiotensin receptor antagonist. The launch strategy includes a dedicated sales force and educational initiatives to drive adoption among nephrologists. Filspari has shown robust efficacy in reducing proteinuria and preserving kidney function in clinical trials.
  • MoAs: Dual endothelin A (ETA) and angiotensin II (AT1) receptor antagonist
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: UC Berkeley GLP-1 Gene Therapy Startup
item.description:
• Summary: A startup spun out from the University of California, Berkeley is developing a gene therapy approach for GLP-1-based treatments. The company leverages jumping genes (transposons) to deliver a GLP-1 analog, aiming for long-lasting expression to treat obesity and type 2 diabetes. This novel platform could offer a sustained alternative to daily GLP-1 injections, potentially improving patient compliance and outcomes.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonism: the gene therapy encodes a GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide that continuously stimulates insulin secretion, slows gastric emptying, and promotes satiety, leading to glycemic control and weight loss.
  • Sources: Endpoints News
